My role is to actively promote cloud-native technologies within the company, advocating for their strategic implementation to enhance our services.  In addition to my project management duties, I am committed to regularly training my colleagues on Kubernetes, Linux, Prometheus, cloud-native ecosystems, and observability practices, ensuring that our team remains at the cutting edge of industry developments.  As a cloud technology enthusiast, I am constantly seeking ways to improve my skills and deepen my knowledge in these technologies, as well as other rapidly evolving fields.

person.name: Bertrand Nau
person.bio: I began my career as a Fullstack Developer with a particular interest in the JVM ecosystem and Rust. Over the years, I have gradually diversified into Cloud, DevOps, and CNCF technologies. Today, in addition to development, I manage Kubernetes clusters, set up observability (LGTM stack), and implement continuous deployment for applications or infrastructure (Ansible, Pulumi, Hashicorp products, etc.).  Passionate about tech and a jack of all trades, I love learning and sharing on a variety of topics, especially at French conferences such as Devoxx FR, Sunny Tech, and Volcamp...

person.name: Dave Altena
person.bio: DevSecOps Engineer who specialises in making secuirty more understandable for Platform Engineers. Always love to learn about new tools and techniques in this area, and love applying them both at home and the workplace. Building https://prlx.eu to help organisations getting their security in check

person.name: Paul Bourhis

I began as a developer, moved into DevOps, and now focus on deploying and operating cloud-native applications. I’m especially interested in Helm chart development and Infrastructure as Code with Terraform, and I enjoy making complex platforms simple to use for development teams.

person.name: Ramiro Alvarez Fernandez
person.bio: Ramiro is a Senior DevOps Engineer with extensive experience in cloud infrastructure, and engineering leadership with solid experience in technologies such as Kubernetes, Elasticsearch, MongoDB. Ramiro's day-to-day involves managing Kubernetes clusters, orchestrating CI/CD pipelines, and overseeing Elasticsearch and MongoDB environments, all while pushing the limits of AWS, GCP, and Azure.  Ramiro is deeply passionate about Cloud FinOps, Observability, and optimizing cloud resource efficiency. Ramiro actively contribute to the cloud native community by sharing insights through presentations at conferences and wrote articles on different sites.

person.name: Seshikanth Guduri
person.bio: I’m a Senior DevOps and platform engineer with 14+ years of experience building reliable, secure, and scalable cloud platforms on AWS, with a focus on Kubernetes, Terraform, and GitLab CI/CD. I’ve led large-scale migrations (200+ VMs) to AWS, cut deployment times by up to 75%, and delivered 99.99% uptime through automation, observability, and SRE practices. As a Kubestronaut, I’m committed to mentoring others and helping teams adopt cloud‑native, GitOps-driven delivery with strong security and governance.

person.name: Kyungjin Park
person.bio: A decade of experience has honed my skills in Kubernetes, leading me to my current role as a Consultant at Broadcom. I am passionate about developing boundary-crossing competencies and ensuring seamless integration of Kubernetes solutions across diverse environments. Proud to be a Kubestronaut!

person.name: Sumir Broota
person.bio: I'm passionate about engineering, learning and finding creative solutions to tech challenges. I have a background in both development and cybersecurity. I am the moderator of BreachForce - a cybersecurity cohort based in Mumbai and I regularly attend various tech events.

person.name: Valentin Klopfenstein
person.bio: I engineer and operate scalable platforms for developers with a strong emphasis on automation and GitOps. I've created and run operators in Kubernetes as well as using Crossplane to provide managed services for customers. When not dabbling with cloud native technologies, I can be found with a soldering iron in my hand, creating my next hardware project.

person.name: Wolfram Gerret Apelt
person.bio: Gerret is a technology enthusiast with over 20 years of experience in IT operations consulting. He got into programming as a teenager, built his first OpenStack private cloud in 2015 and thinks of documentation as an art-form. He thrives in architectural roles that require vision and challenge his deep technical expertise. He is employed as a Senior Operations Engineer with IS Nordic A/S where he designs, implements and operates strong Kubernetes clusters both on-premise and in the cloud, for clients as well as as in-house workloads.

person.name: Itay Shakury
person.bio: Itay Shakury is VP Open Source at Aqua Security, where he leads engineering and strategy of open source, cloud native, security solutions. Itay has some 20 years of professional experience in various software development, architecture and product management roles. Itay had initiated several local technical communities and events, including the successful CNCF Tel Aviv meetup group.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm honored to be recognized as a CNCF Ambassador, and join this amazing team of worldwide experts. To me, the CNCF is an intersection between two passions of mine - technology and community, which I have been pursuing since early in my career. I'm excited to continue to collaborate with the various technical working groups, events, projects and initiatives that the CNCF ecosystem continuously generates.

person.name: Mike Conrad
person.bio: I am a solutions driven professional with 10+years of experience in the tech industry. I possess a deep knowledge of containerization including Docker, and Kubernetes. My wealth of experience across a number of disciplines and proven ability to deliver solutions for customers sets me apart. I am well versed in cloud technologies having primary experience with Azure and AWS.

person.name: Przemysław Rathnow
person.bio: Doing Muay Thai and DevOps since I was 14. Do you think you can beat me?  I work as much as I can, as hard as I can I know more than I think I do  Road to Golden Kubeastronaut!

I design systems that are not only fast and secure but also easy and enjoyable to use. I love sharing what I've learned, helping wherever I can, and speaking about my work.

If you’re up for chatting about Kubernetes or exploring the ever-changing world of DevOps, let’s connect and make the complicated things a bit more fun together!

person.name: Bruno Vieira Pessoa

My background spans networking, compute, storage, and virtualization across major vendors in the enterprise IT landscape. I aim to bridge the gap between hardware and software by building scalable, efficient, and reliable platforms that support modern application delivery.

person.name: Stanislav Kolenkin

My professional achievements are numerous certificates, including SEI Software Architecture Professional Certificate, Kubernetes, AWS, GCP Certifications, and many others. Along with his outstanding architect skills and vast industry knowledge, I am proficient in critical problem-solving and excels in dealing with non-standard production issues.

person.name: Gijs Middelkamp
person.bio: Started in 2008 as a Software Developer and Linux Engineer. After a job switch in 2015 I grew to be a DevOps and Platform engineer position at my current company. We use Kubernetes as our own platform, but I also am one of the engineers who developed our own Kubernetes as a Service platform (PKE). I get energy from improving setups, training people and help others adopt the cloud-native thought.

person.name: Girirajan Soundar Rajan
person.bio: Girirajan Soundarrajan is a Senior Software Engineer at Adobe, specializing in platform engineering, Kubernetes-driven cloud-native infrastructure, and platform reliability. He has deep expertise in Infrastructure, database architecture, cluster security, observability, and large-scale distributed systems running on AWS and Azure. His work spans CNCF technologies, including Kubernetes, Prometheus, Cilium, Docker, Grafana, Istio, and cloud-native messaging systems, enabling resilient and scalable production environments. He is passionate about continuous learning and advancing expertise across the Kubernetes ecosystem.

person.name: Hanzala Mohammed
person.bio: I'm a DevOps & Platform Engineer based in West Yorkshire, with a focus on Kubernetes security, GitOps, and cloud-native architecture. I enjoy simplifying complex topics and have been creating hands-on video walkthroughs to help others upskill for the CKS and broader CNCF ecosystem.  My interests lie in platform reliability, open source tools, and practical DevSecOps implementations. I’m also an active contributor on LinkedIn and YouTube where I share insights, live labs, and breakdowns of exam-focused Kubernetes challenges.

person.name: Ivan Ballatore

person.bio: Site Reliability Engineer at RingCentral

person.name: Tada Kim
person.bio: I am a Site Reliability Engineer who is passionate about learning new things and has a keen interest in cloud-native solutions and open source technologies.

person.name: Afzal Ansari
person.bio: Afzal is a passionate open-source enthusiast with a deep interest in cloud-native technologies and building new things on top of them. He specializes in supporting cloud infrastructure and actively contributes to ArgoCD open-source project. Additionally, he has made significant contributions to various CNCF projects, including Meshery, Keptn, and Jaeger. His dedication to the Keptn project earned him recognition as a Keptn Crew member. He also enjoys creating video content and writing blogs to share his experiences in the cloud-native space with like-minded individuals.

person.name: Dean Shorter
person.bio: Dean is a Senior Linux / DevOps Engineer with over 20 years of experience designing secure, scalable, and fault-tolerant cloud-native platforms across multiple industries. He specializes in Kubernetes, AWS, and Infrastructure as Code, with deep expertise in building and securing distributed systems in hybrid and multi-cloud environments. Dean has architected large-scale, microservices-based solutions supporting millions of users globally and led complex cloud migration initiatives. Passionate about automation and cloud-native security, he continues to experiment with Kubernetes and distributed systems through hands-on lab environments and real-world platform engineering challenges.
